issues.title: Match OPENAI_BASE_URL with OpenAI
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**

I would like Spring's OPENAI_BASE_URL related parameters to end with /v1, possibly for migration ease, check to see if it already ends in v1 first.

**Current Behavior**

Right now, the settings named similar to OPENAI_BASE_URL are different than canonical openai clients, such as the python client, as they are not rooted at v1.

**Context**

This creates problems for those mixing between different client libraries, as in spring ai, you can't have /v1 whereas all the others must have /v1

Here's one example of openai, but almost all libraries follow this also. https://github.com/openai/openai-python/blob/dfc4cfabfab3db89c0668c9bfd5f3f6f49093935/README.md?plain=1#L712

comments.body:
Spring AI allows setting both `base-url` and specific paths (e.g., `completions-path` for chat models), so you have full flexibility to configure it as needed. Check out the docs for details:  

- [Connection Properties](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_connection_properties)  
- [Configuration Properties](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html#_configuration_properties)

what I'm looking for is a way to have env support so that we don't have to join two properties to achieve configuration parity with how `OPENAI_BASE_URL` is used canonically, particularly not writing code to merge things together especially since openai works rooted at v1

Then how about try this option: `spring.ai.openai.chat.completions-path`?
Have you tried it?

The goal is to have the env variable OPENAI_BASE_URL read by spring, which defaults to a URL that ends in v1 https://github.com/openai/openai-python?tab=readme-ov-file#configuring-the-http-client

So for example, to override for ollama, in all their clients, you set this
```
OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
```

same thing for the many others that emulate openai's api

Given that, I'm not sure what you mean to say. For example, are you suggesting to strip /v1 from `spring.ai.openai.chat.completions-path`? e.g. setting it to `/chat/completions` instead of `/v1/chat/completions`?

That is a workaround, but it also doesn't directly support the ask, which is to make autoconfiguration that honors `OPENAI_BASE_URL` as it is defined.

Here's the current workaround to use canonical ENV vars based on this advice
```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    openai:
      base-url: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:https://api.openai.com/v1}
      api-key: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:enter-your-api-key}
      chat:
        # ensure /v1 isn't appended twice
        completions-path: /chat/completions
```

ps one thing this will hit later is embeddings, etc, so I'm mainly asking for a feature that just works like normal openai does, and out of the box *even if* we still support the spring-specific mechanics and endpoint-specific prefixes. Basically, I don't see the value in having people update potentially several config items to rebase them over a common v1 prefix, esp since it is defined by openai and this is for openai

 cc @ThomasVitale

something easy to forget is that openai's api is mounted and defined at v1.. all apis are below that. hence this is the base of the api https://github.com/openai/openai-openapi/blob/master/openapi.yaml#L15

That is a good point. Considering that the implementations across other languages and frameworks define the base URL with the `/v1` suffix (including other Java implementations), I would consider doing the same in Spring AI.

Furthermore, some "OpenAI-compatible" providers might use the same path (e.g. `/chat/completion`), but not necessarily the `/v1` part since that's not officially part of the endpoint (see https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2251). In that case, it would be useful to be able to change just the base path instead of having to change the paths also for completion, embedding, moderation, image, transcription, and audio just to remove the `/v1` prefix.

When using autoconfiguration, each of those paths must be changed once. But when not using autoconfiguration, the same changes are needed whenever an OpenAI client is instantiated, increasing the number of customizations needed.

When using autoconfiguration, each of those paths must be changed once. But when not using autoconfiguration, the same changes are needed whenever an OpenAI client is instantiated, increasing the number of customizations needed.
going to close this as I don't see any active interest in resolving it

issues.title: Documentation: "spring.data.redis.uri" must be "spring.data.redis.url"
issues.body:
The correct property name is _[url](https://github.com/CyangGitHub/springBoot/blob/ffd35aaf5f0fd3e7b9ad5618201ea639acd3edbc/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/data/redis/RedisProperties.java#L42)_, not _uri_. 

Document to review: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blame/bed1db352a44da91d4a3f1bf2383cbaf5e361247/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/vectordbs/redis.adoc#L87
